text:
the fire made a major run to the north late sunday, forcing the evacuation of fort greely and part of delta junction, also temporarily closing the richardson highway. winds gusting to 50+ mph, pushed flames past a fire break and onto the main post area of fort greely. the evacuation of fort greely was ordered at 1030, by the post commander. about midnight the continuous winds forced the evacuation of residents in the southern part of delta junction. the fire jumped the richardson highway and destroyed a residential structure,(stam) was assigned to the fire. crews are working to contain spot fires and secure the fires perimeter. in addition to resources listed there are 4 dozers, 3 nodwells, and 2 fire medics units assigned to this fire.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Donnelly Flats Fire (AK) across 4 reporting days through 1999-06-14: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
cool temperatures and favorable humidity have minimized fire activity and aided firefighters with mop-up. efforts were directed to areas of heat concentration and gridding all accessable parts of the line. smokejumpers and crews rehabilitated handline and the tracked excavator continued to perform exceptionally well with rehabilitation of the dozer line. gridding located fire hot spots within 300' of the control lines. three crews and the 16 smokejumpers were released at the end of the shift. their demobe is scheduled for 5/24. the fire was controlled at 2100 hrs 5/23.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
land & water, sta higher humidities and cloudcover kept fire activity to a minimum. burnouts planned for 2 allotments were put on hold, but will be left in the plan if conditions permit. two type ii crews (minto & tanana) were released today. one light helicopter was reassigned to fire b466,as well as an aerial firing module. one type 1 crew is slated for release tomorrow, leaving the fire with one type 1 crew and a medium helicopter(523eh) with helitack module.

text:
doyon, nca the fire received some precipitation today which helped meet containment objectives. the main slop over is 100% mopped up and is currently being monitored. good progress was made on the left flank with hose and most smokes were mopped up. the fire was contained today at 1800, control is estimated for 7/17 at 2200. one type 2 crew (noorvik) is being released on sunday, 7/18, 1800. i.c. is looking to probeye the fire on sunday. a monday demob is planned but contingent upon probeye mission.

text:
northern field office zone detection reported that the fire area was clear enough to accurately map, thus the reduction in acreage. the fire had hot areas on the southeast side where it was actively backing. it was smoking at the mouth of franklin creek, on the north side of uhler creek and on the south side of hilda creek.

text:
bankhead complex on the national forests in alabama - all 24 fires are contained. 2 new starts today that were held to 1 acre by prompt action by the engines. state of south carolina dozers and engines released today at request of forestry commission.90's and relative humidity in the 30's. mill creek fire was contained today. forsyth fire had flame in the interior today - 6bl dropped several loads of water on this fire.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
fire activity decreased considerably, consisting mostly of isolated torching throughout the fire and limited spread on the northeast side.crews made considerable progress in cold-trailing and mopping-up in divisions a, c, and e.1998 and essentially halted for the day. an attempt was made to gps the fire perimeter, but the effort failed. another attempt will occur tomorrow. the fire has numerous fingers difficult to map from the ground. additional resources received for the fire will allow release of additional initial attack forces back to the south zone.

text:
fire buring in pinion junipter and logging slash. managed under a modified suppression plan. fire ended up being at 305 acres. rate of spread has stopped by burning into natural barries androads. the fire is being monitored by red butte and bill williams lookouts on the kaibab national forest. had some smokes show up this morning.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Cataract Fire (AZ) across 3 reporting days through 1999-06-30: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
incident consists of 3 separate fires being managed for resource benefits. the redtail fire (declared out on 10/8 at 81 acres), the boundary (358 acres), and the camelot (1009 acres). only minimal activity remains on the boundary fire with a few heavies still smoldering in the interior. the camelot fire continues to exhibit moderate fire behavior with movement over the rim into the main canyon extending approximately 1500 feet below the rim. the fire still shows potential to move laterally to the west below the rim. camelot and boundary fires are being monitored daily by local personnel. all additional resources have been demobed.

text:
transition of the fire to the local agency will occur 6/2 at 0600. resources remaining on the fire will include two type 2 crews, one type 3 helicopter, one engine, and one water tender. the entire fire was within category i desert tortoise habitat. increased acres and perimeter calculated by arcview gis based on ground reports & gps flight.acres burned: blm - 12,158 state - 3,822 lake havasu f.o. - 15,132phoenix f.o. - 848 yuma co. - 11,817 yavapai co. - 4,163 and wilderness - 4,575 controlled 0600 6/03

text:
***fire weather watch for gusty n-nw winds 12-25 mph, w/ lowering rh to 12-25%.*** crews continue to mop up along fire lines. priority is hot springs in div. a. night shift to consist of two engine patrol and mop up. base camp to be discontinued by 1200 hours tomorrow (friday 10/8/1999). forest to take over the incident at 1800 hours tomorrow. incident will be left with 10 crews and local engines for mop up. also, 2 helicopters will remain on the incident.

text:
10 fs handcrews and 1 strike team of engines completed line and began rehab. helicopters continued to make water drops on hotspots on islands within burn perimeter. demob continues and incident will be turned over to the nps at 1800 6/1/99. 4 handcrews, 3 helicoters and 2 airtankers to remain on the incident. juniper fire declared out 0600 5/31/99

text:
the east fire became it's own managed incident 2100hrs 07/27/99.the hpc complex is working the five inc/50ac; lightening/35ac; lake inc/5ac; snag cub/10ac; stein/25ac. the stein is a very dangerous, snag infested incident. four injuries - one needing a medivac, late 07/26. two knee injuries and one heat exhaustion.07/27 - one heat exhaustion

text:
type i incident management team, california team 2, with studebaker as the incident commander has been activated for the stein fire. the fire is located within the marble mountain wilderness area of the klamath national forest. the fire is burning in heavy concentrations of snags and downed timber from the yellow incident of 1987. thirty-seven smokejumpers from the redding base were assigned to contain spot fires with the support of type 1 and 2 helicopters. 8 federal type 1 crews are also working the numerous spot fires. limited suppression action is being taken on the main fire due to the extreme hazardous conditions. heavy smoke hampered air operations afternoon of 090899 (smoke dense up to 11,000ft) * confined by natural barriers or line construction in areas of low risk meeting minimum suppression methods= *"30% confined".

text:
the fire is burning on catalina island, usn hovercraft are shuttling resource to catalina island from long beach california. on 07/22 at 1600 a spot fire outside of the line burned an additional 200 acres. current threat: watershed and the airport in the sky. control problems: island with little firefighting resources and inaccessable terrain

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Goat Ca Lac 99119650 Fire (CA) across 3 reporting days through 1999-07-23: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
north:fire cont to spread nw downhill and up the carmel river drainage. the heavy smoke cont to hinder air ops & recon opportunities to assess the situation. the carmel river and los padres reservoir provides domestic water to the monterey peninsula. local political concerns center on the carmel river watershed. ventana wilderness values, and cont threat to structures. loss of resources due to 21-day restrictions continues to hinder staffing efforts.south:favorable cond allowed for firing ops cont on the wnw flanks. the fire made a strong run up the north fork big creek against the wnw line & was controlled with air support and ground crews. the fire cont to back into the big creek and devils canyon areas with structure protection and perimeter control being provided. the t&e species (steelhead trout and red legged frog) remain a critical concern in the devils canyon and big creek areas. slow replacement of lost resources due to 21-day restrictions continues to threaten staffing needs.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
the pine and boles fire joined yesterday and are reported as one fire.the pine fire is 22,272 acres and the yellow was contained yesterday (8/28/99 at 1800 hrs.) and is 4,669 ac. a major burnout operation was conducted on the pine fire to reinforce seven miles of dozerline. by 1700 hours approximately 6,500 ac. of unburned area remained between the existing fire perimeter and the reinforced fireline. this area will be burned during night operations. the reported fire acreage for the pine fire includes approximately 500 ac. that was burned during operations today.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
six lightning fires caused fires north and west of lake oroville. due to erratic fire behavior and limited resources, structures, recreational sites and private improvements around little grass valley reservior continued to be threatened. the hibbard and china fires were both controlled. the mosquito ridge fire was contained today with expected control on 9/1. burning operations were completed on the bean ck and devils gap. containment is expected on 9/2 on the devils and bean ck. hand and dozer lines continue to be constructed and improved. the stag point fire is expected to be staffed on 8/31 and helispots have been identified. fires:mosquito ridge=50, hibbard creek=8, bean creek=1435, stag=480, china=2, devils gap=1480

text:
the three un-contained fires in the complex (bucks,lookout, and piegon) continue to be active in several areas during day shift. good progress was made on indirect line on the lookout wich is expected to be completed and burned out tonight. the threat to structures continue in the hwy 70 corridor north of bucks fire. chrom fire-(110ac. controlled) rock(5ac. controlled) spanish (0.2ac. controlled) claremont (172 ac. contained) hambly (0.5 ac. out) king (0.7 ac. out) bucks 7,100 ac.) bald (1 ac.) piedgon (3,250 ac.) lookout (2,170 ac.) horton-2 (4,336 ac. contained)the threats continue to be additional acreage of private land, structures, timber sales, bio mass operations on national forest lands, historic and prehistoric sites on several fires, spotted owl habitat, powerhouse and and a game refuge with inaccessible terrian.

text:
willow - cdf team 9, kerrigan as ic with unified command art porter, blm ic. lost all dozer lines on east and north side. objective today is to re-establish those lines. a structure protection group is located at the hagata ranch (historic site). fire is 5000-6000 acres in the willow creek valley and has jumped state route 139. current threats include timber, rangeland and archeological sites.state route 139 is closed from 1 mile north to 30 miles north of susanville at south grasshopper road and 23.1 miles south of adin at termo-grasshopper road. detours are available.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Willow - Lmu 2731 Fire (CA) across 4 reporting days through 1999-08-26: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
control efforts are focusing on divisions b, c and d down to mill creek. this morning 18 crews, totaling 270 firefighters were flown to this fireline. many of these firefighters are working control lines across mill creek canyon. the control lines are in extremely steep country in a wilderness study area. div c line is from the l line road on the north (mill creek rim) down to mill creek and from the k line road (lassen trail) down to mill creek on the south side. divisions c and d have been reinforced with wildland hoselays. once brushing of both lines has been completed, a firing operation is proposed to provide containment by 10-05-99. strengthening of the power line road in divisions m and n continues. efforts are still underway to tie the powerline road with the lassen trail and mill creek. evacuation orders have been lifted for the residents of the community of boondock.

text:
the lowden fire started on 7/2 at approximately 1330 hrs. from an escape blm prescribe burn at the lowden ranch. the fire threatened the community of lewiston, ca. during it's initial runs. twentythree homes and three outbuildings were confirmed lost. control problems continue to be changing wind direction, unburned fuels within the fire perimeter and steep slopes.

text:
cdf major incident command team (#1 stovall) has command of the incident. the winds subsided last night and brought with it higher humidities and lower temperatures. several local government strike teams arrived to reinforce our contingency plan of protecting the communities of hidden valley and berryessa estates.

text:
rock - 700 acres burning 3 miles south of shingletown. structures in shingletown and manton were threatened. interior islands have hot spots appearing. containment expected tonight. 10 miles of line built, 3 miles remaining to build. ict 4 (johnson) is supporting fire suppression efforts.east - containment expected tonight. 288 acres 2 miles east of french gulch in steep brushy terrain. heavy mop-up today with hot spots in the interior.4 (johnson) is supporting fire suppression efforts.mammoth fire - 9 fires/400 acres burning 2 miles west of shasta lake in steep terrain. threats to historical structures and timber. 40-50% contained. 3 fires at 100 acres each, 4 fires uncontained. crews will be ferried in by boats to base of fire.

text:
the fire started about 1045 hours on 9/26 south of redding in the vicinity of canyon road and happy valley road. the fire spread rapidly pushed by strong north to northeast winds. many homes were being threatened. there have been 51 homes lost with 26 more damaged. additionally 124 outbuildings, 1 commercial were also confirmed lost. all electric, water and gas services have been restored. all schools are open and the red cross shelter has been closed.

text:
a member of the l.a. county fire department inmate crew suffered a fatal injury while the crews were walking at night in steep rugged, terrain. it is believed the crew member lost his footing fell a 100 feet down a steep embankment.structures and livestock continue to be threatened in the fire area. a nightime firing operation completed a 800 acre burnout.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
area and containment data will be adjusted in next 209 to reflect additional fire activity caused by spot fires in drainage west of division z. an estimated 1500 acres were consumed due to spot fires that occurred at approximately 1600 hours. extreme fire behavior spotting distances over 1/2 mile and strong erratic winds are making containment difficult. incident resources have been moved to address the expansion. several ranch structures communications equipment at the plowshare peak communications site, and firefighter safety are priorities. control lines on the south and east side have been burned out and are holding. cooperators sbc, ccc, cdf, usfs,blm, smr, krn, stb, lac, crp, sbso, chp, afv, nws, lvn, avf, lvn, dgs, knp, cnc.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Spanish Ca Sbc 4140 Fire (CA) across 6 reporting days through 1999-07-23: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
unified command (foster/young) with type 1 and 2 imt (usfs/cdf). current threat is to national recreation area lands, water quality and tes habitat. containment appears to be progressing on schedule, with burnout proceeding when suitable weather and fire behavior conditions exist. spike camp at shasta dam campground will be broken down at the end of 9/4. rehabilitation of fire suppression activities is being done when it will not jeopardize control lines. beneficial use is being made of the shasta county sheriff lake patrol boats to transport fire suppression personnel to drop points. explosive fireline construction crew is making progress on control lines. control problems remain steep topography, long travel times and indirect line construction. fire potential to 40,000 acres at control.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the High Complex Fire (CA) across 11 reporting days through 1999-09-03: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
the complex consists of 2 fires: onion/16,602(94% cont) and megram/35,105(20% cont). the complex is being co-managed by a type 1 eastern imt (dubois, ic) and the type 2 orca imt (weltch, ic). the onion fire has experienced some reburns in isolated portions of the fire, but it is still holding within existing control lines of 2,885 chains. the megram fire has combined with the fawn fire and is still burning actively. on the east, fire continues to burn in slide cr drainage towards control lines. eagle cr drainage is burning out towards control lines. quimby cr and new river drainages have been tied together and lines are holding. eight mile cr drainage continues to burn to the north. active fire in the trinity mountain area continues to threaten the six rivers nf in the red cap and horse linto drainages. the fire also continues to threaten the klamath nf to the north. other threats are to structures, key watershed, salmon/steelhead habitat, late successional reserves and wilderness.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
crews continue to make good progress on line reinforcement and mop-up operations. a high probability for reburn and spotting continues with a forcasted wind event expected tonight through saturday. current threats are to the trinity alps wilderness, private timber lands, harvesting operations and residential structures.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
four fires are currently being managed for resource benefits in the mokelumne, emigrant, and carson-iceberg wilderness. the carmoke complex is being managed by forest fire use management incident commander(cones).minimal fire growth due to heavy cloud cover and light rain.stage iii fire assessment has been completed and signed for the hiram fire.deer fire = 312hiram fire= 130douglas = 1.5red can = .25

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Carmoke Fire (CA) across 4 reporting days through 1999-08-23: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
burn out operations and line construction progressed very well yesterday evening and last night. mopup operations are progressing well. ir flights show hot areas near the lines in the northeast and southeast areas of the fire. todays forecast for hot and dry weather creates a concern that interior burn out and reburns could spot across the lines. secondary lines and structure groups are in place to address the potential threats of the fire.highway 120 will reopen today using traffic escorts.additional threats; goshawk and spotted owl habitat, old growth timber stands, city of san francisco power facility, evergreen lodge, sawmill tract, peach growers tract, and camp mather. the fire is causing losses to the local lodge, rafting and recreational businesses as many activities have been scheduled in the area for the holiday weekend.demobilization of excess resources will begin today.

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
the fire is being managed under a unified command with usfs ca. type 1 team, don studebaker as the ic and cdf incident commander tony clarabut. the red flag warning has expired. it will continue to dry the next several days. conditions continue to cause active dangerous fire behavior and control problems. the fire changed directions yesterday and is now burning to the north. the fire continues to threaten structures, property and stock. numerous unburned spots and fingers remain within the perimeter.

text:
conditions were favorable today for the planned burnout operations. predicted thundercells have not materialize at this time. all indirect line may have at least some black next to it by the end of the burning period. further burnout and widening of black line will take place tomorrow as conditions allow.fire was flown with gps late last evening. acreage increase due to more accurate mapping and burnout operations

text:
continuing to have new smokes pop up all over the area. reburning all over due to fresh dead pine straw. burning across water at times where fuels are present. added 7 more acres due to more areas that burned yesterday. new areas started reburning yesterday that have not shown any fire in weeks. in process of ordering replacement engine for the two we have for monday.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Ferry Complex Fire (FL) across 7 reporting days through 1999-03-05: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
fires continue to burn actively in afternoons due to low humidities, moderate winds and persisting drought conditions. interior swamp areas are continuing to burnout and cause spotting, hampering control efforts. air tankers were utilized to help ground resources control spot fires on the north flank. 75% (6 miles) of the secondary control lines adjacent to private property has been completed. this complex now includes the cow house fire (6765 acres) and the owl creek fire (2624 acres).

source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
the fire started apx. 1500 4/20, cause is arson. burnout operations to existing roads has contained the fire. higher humidities today provided good mopup conditions. florida division of forestry, jefferson smufritt corp, baker county fire & sheriff departments contributed to containment and continue to support the incident.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Cypress Creek Fire (FL) across 4 reporting days through 1999-04-23: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
containment lines have been established around the area of the fire south of fl. hwy 2. burnout operations to improve and secure lines are continuing in the nw and sw areas of the fire south of hwy. 2. hwy. 2 and hwy. 94 remain closed due to heavy smoke and safety concerns. air space over the fire continues to be restricted. the fire is within 7 to 8 miles of fl. hwy. 441 near the rural community of benton, fl, and 8 miles of council, ga. helicopters and air tankers continue to drop water and retardant on this area of the fire to slow its progress and reinforce lines. firefighting efforts in the okefenokee nwr continue to concentrate on maintaining and enhancing fire breaks on the swamp's edge to keep the fire within the swamp. contingency plans for structural protection are under development for council, ga, and deep creek, fl. acreage total includes perimeter from burnout operations, some unburned interior may exist.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Friendly Fire (FL) across 8 reporting days through 1999-05-31: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
this fire was called controlled on 7/21 1500. yesterday strong winds pushed the fire across control lines increasing 12 additional acres for a total of 227 at 90% controlled. kbdi at ocala is nearing 500 and has become the driest july in 20 years. two engines with modules were ordered today to assist with initial attack.one new fire on the osceola rd called hog house. fire is 1 acre at this time. half the fire is in big gum swamp wilderness. kbdi at osceola is 693 with only .02 inches of rain since 7/24 increasing potential for severe activity. two lightning caused fires on 7/21 still smoldering with active afternoon flames on the osceola. one lightning caused fire on the seminole rd controlled at 1 acre.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Bill Branch Fire (FL) across 4 reporting days through 1999-07-27: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
islands of unburned fuel in the interior and some near the lines continue to burn. ponds are reburning very well. reburning in the duff areas is igniting vegetation that was not consumed the first time. the strong winds are felling trees in the bay where all the duff is gone, leaving all lateral roots exposed up to two feet in height.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Richard Fire (FL) across 3 reporting days through 1999-06-08: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
the fire was flown before noon today and six (6) total smokes were observed. scattered showers are moving over the fire area. aerial monitoring will continue and 209 reports will be submitted if the fire activity increases.209s will be submitted until further notice. cost update will be reflected on the final 209.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Okr Friendly Fire Fire (GA) across 5 reporting days through 1999-06-16: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
this lightning caused fire, burning in the selway bitteroot wilderness is being managed as a wildland fire utilized for resource benefit (wfu). it is burning in subalpine fir/lodgepole pine and duff/litter. monitoring of this fire is being accomplished using aerial reconnaisance and ground personnel. current threats are to an outfitter camp and three forest service trails. little activity today in spite of high winds due to high humidities and some precipitation.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Colt Killed Creek Fire (ID) across 15 reporting days through 1999-09-25: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
this lightning caused fire on the clearwater nf is being managed as a wildfire being utilized for resource benefit (wfu). fire size has increased from 20 acres to 138 acres since 9/14. limited suppression action is being taken on the east flank to protect an outfitter's camp located approximately 1 mile from the fire.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Potato Fire (ID) across 9 reporting days through 1999-09-27: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
crews are mopping up the perimeter. all firelines are in and crews are making good progress with water being pumped around the perimeter. weather dry and hot but stable with little wind.using heavy equipment to break up berms and take apart log decks. expected to transition back to a class iii team on tuesday july 13th.estimated losses shown above are from approximately 300 mbf of timber at $500 per for $150000.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Canyon Cr Fire (ID) across 3 reporting days through 1999-07-11: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
structures, the idaho national engineering and environmental laboratory (department of energy), powerlines and part of the oregon trail are threatened. loss of habitat for sage grouse is a primary environmental concern. control problems include low relative humidities, gusty winds and high temperatures. costs to date exclude aircraft.a strong type iii organization is in place.demobilization has been halted at this time due to a predicted fire weather watch on 7-24.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Cedar Butte Fire (ID) across 4 reporting days through 1999-07-23: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
this fire will be managed in a confine strategy and will utilize natural barriers to confine the fire's spread within the ballinger drainage on the west and pinchot drainage on the east. the fire's progress will be monitored with field observers and aircraft. fire size has increased by 5 acres in the last 2 days and is now reported to 207 acres.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Pinchot Fire (ID) across 3 reporting days through 1999-07-26: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
this lightning caused fire burning in the selway bitterroot wilderness is being managed as wildland fire utilized for resource benefit (wfu) incident. the fire is burning in scattered timber and brush. 4 smokejumpers have been committed to this fire to accomplish limited suppression on one flank of the fire and protect an outfitter's camp. fire activity has increased the past 2 days due to warm temperatures and low relative humidity.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Gettysburg Fire (ID) across 11 reporting days through 1999-09-25: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
this lightning caused fire, burning in the selway bitterroot wilderness, is being managed for resource benefit (wfu). the fire is burning actively in heavy timber and brush. current threat is to a wilderness trail. 2 personnel are assigned to the fire to monitor the activity and keep the trail system open. the fire activity has been minimal the past 2 days.

Daily situation-report values — acres burned, percent contained, and total personnel — for the Low Roller Fire (ID) across 4 reporting days through 1999-09-23: <ts></ts>
source: figshare.com/articles/19858927 (St. Denis et al. 2023, ICS-209-PLUS, CC BY 4.0)

text:
district aerially ignited approximately 1500 acres on 9/8. fire was burning within prescription until the passage of a dry cold front on 9/23 through 9/25 that brought west winds gusting to 50 mph. wfsa completed 9/25. aerial recon was flown on the fire today, 10/01. fire perimeter outside of planned burn units now is estimated at 1072 acres. fire received precip 9/26&9/27 which lessened fire activity. fire continues to burn actively in unburned islands within the perimeter. helicopter bucket work is being used to check the fire spread at the perimeters.
